plan: Step 1: Create `src/domain/url-seed.ts` — a pure module with two functions: (a) `readSeedFromUrl(): string | null` that parses `window.location.search` for the `seed` query parameter and returns it if present, or null; (b) `writeSeedToUrl(seed: string): void` that calls `window.history.replaceState()` to set `?seed=<value>` without triggering a page reload. Keep these thin wrappers over browser APIs so the logic is testable and isolated.

Step 2: Create `src/domain/resolve-seed.ts` — a pure function `resolveSeed(rawSeed: string | null): string` that takes the raw URL value and returns a valid seed. If `rawSeed` is non-null and passes `isValidSeed()`, return it as-is. Otherwise, call `generateSeed()` and return the new seed. This is the single decision point for seed validity.

Step 3: Wire seed initialization into `src/main.ts` — at the top of the existing `if (app)` block, call `readSeedFromUrl()`, pass the result to `resolveSeed()`, then call `writeSeedToUrl()` with the resolved seed. Store the resolved seed in a `const` for later use by generators (future tickets). This ensures every page load ends with a valid `?seed=` in the URL bar.

Step 4: Write unit tests in `tests/us-005-url-seed.test.ts` covering: (a) `resolveSeed` returns the input when valid, generates a new seed when input is null, and generates a new seed when input is invalid (empty string, special chars, spaces); (b) `readSeedFromUrl` correctly parses `?seed=abc123` and returns null when param is missing; (c) `writeSeedToUrl` calls `history.replaceState` with the correct URL; (d) integration: reading a written seed round-trips correctly; (e) determinism: same seed value always resolves to itself.
Step 5: Run quality gates — `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` — and fix any issues. All existing tests (US-001 through US-006) must continue to pass alongside the new US-005 tests.
testing_recommendations: Unit test `resolveSeed()` with valid seeds (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores), invalid seeds (empty string, spaces, special characters like `!@#$%`), and null input — verify valid seeds pass through unchanged and invalid/missing seeds produce a new valid seed.
Unit test `readSeedFromUrl()` by mocking `window.location.search` with values like `?seed=abc123`, `?seed=`, `?other=value`, and empty string — verify correct extraction or null return.
Unit test `writeSeedToUrl()` by spying on `window.history.replaceState` — verify it is called with the expected URL containing `?seed=<value>` and preserves the existing pathname.
Round-trip test: write a seed to URL, then read it back — verify the same seed is returned.
Run all existing test suites (US-001 through US-006) to confirm no regressions from the new code or main.ts changes.

Manual browser test: load the page without `?seed=` and verify a seed appears in the URL bar; copy the URL, open in a new tab, and verify the same seed is preserved; manually set an invalid seed like `?seed=!!!` and verify it is replaced with a valid one on load.
risks: Browser API mocking in Vitest: `window.location` and `window.history.replaceState` need careful mocking/stubbing in a Node-based test runner. Mitigation: use `vi.stubGlobal` or a small URL helper that can be injected for testing.

Future tickets (US-007+) expect `main.ts` to expose the resolved seed — the wiring pattern chosen now sets the contract. Mitigation: keep the seed as a simple exported const or a thin `getActiveSeed()` accessor so later tickets can import it without refactoring.

Edge case: if another query parameter exists alongside `seed`, `writeSeedToUrl` must preserve it. Mitigation: use `URLSearchParams` to manipulate only the `seed` key and reconstruct the full query string.
